rounds.prompt: How many times longer is the Great Wall of China than Hadrian's Wall?
rounds.fact: Hadrian's Wall is ~138 km long; the Great Wall stretches ~21,196 km — 154× longer, and took centuries to build.

rounds.prompt: How many times larger is the Roman Empire (at peak) than modern Italy?
rounds.fact: Italy is ~301,000 km²; the Roman Empire at its peak covered ~5 million km² — about 17× larger (core territory; ~57× by some estimates including provinces).

rounds.prompt: How many times taller is the Pyramid of Giza than the average Egyptian man of that era?
rounds.fact: Ancient Egyptians averaged ~1.6 m; the Great Pyramid originally stood 146 m — 91× taller. Imagine stacking 91 people.

rounds.prompt: How many times heavier is the Great Wall of China than the Empire State Building?
rounds.fact: The Empire State Building weighs ~365,000 tonnes; the Great Wall is estimated at ~374 billion kg — over 1,000× heavier.

rounds.prompt: How many times longer was the Byzantine Empire than the United States has existed?
rounds.fact: The USA has existed ~250 years; the Byzantine Empire lasted ~1,123 years (330–1453 AD) — 4.5× longer.
